title: Percentages, Financial Maths, Rates, and Time — PYP Mastery

questions.question: Mrs. Smith bought 150 bagels for 54 cents each. She sold them all for 85 cents each. Calculate her total profit.
questions.explanation:
Profit per bagel = $85 - 54 = 31$ cents. Total profit = $150 \times 31 = 4650$ cents = 

$46.50$ [20, 21].

**Common mistake:** The student might provide the profit in cents (4650) without converting to dollars, or only calculate the total sales amount.

questions.question: In 2019, Patrick had a salary of $$32000$. This was an increase of2on his salary in 2017. Calculate his salary in 2017 to the nearest cent.$
questions.explanation:
Let 2017 salary be $x$. $1.09x = 32000 \rightarrow x = 32000 ÷ 1.09 \approx2[22, 23].$

**Common mistake:** Students often calculate $9\%$ of^32000 and subtract it, resulting in

$$291202

questions.question: Edward invests $$204$ for four years at a rate of2per year compound interest. Calculate, to the nearest cent, how much money he has at the end of these four years.$
questions.explanation:
$Amount = 204 \times (1.06)^4 = 204 \times 1.26247... = 257.5452..., which rounds to$. $257.55$ [22, 23].

**Common mistake:** Rounding early in the calculation could lead to $$257.54$, which is slightly inaccurate.$

questions.question: A plane flies^2730 km from Paris to Istanbul. It departs Paris at $10:23$ and arrives in Istanbul at $15:48$. Given that Istanbul time is 2 hours ahead of Paris, calculate the average speed of the plane.
questions.explanation:
Duration = Arrival time - Departure time - Time difference. $15:48 - 10:23 = 5$ hours 25 minutes. Subtract 2 hours = 3 hours 25 minutes. $3$ hours^25 mins = $3 + 25/60 = 3.4166...$ hours. Speed = $2730 ÷ 3.4166... \approx 799$ km/h [23, 24].

**Common mistake:** Students often forget to subtract the time difference, using 5 hours 25 minutes for the calculation.

questions.question: Halima sells apples for $$0.45$ per kilogram. She reduces this price by2. Calculate the new price per kilogram.$
questions.explanation:
Reduction = $20\%$ of 

$0.45 = 0.2 \times 0.45 = 0.09. New price =$

0.45 -0.09 = 0.36 [23, 25].$$
**Common mistake:** The student might provide the amount of reduction (
$$0.09$) instead of the new price.$
